name: GPS Exchange Format (GPX)
spec: GPX is an XML format rooted at <gpx version="1.1"> in the Topografix namespace. Routes use <rte>/<rtept>; recorded tracks use <trk> containing one or more <trkseg>, each a sequence of <trkpt lat=.. lon=..> points. A track point may carry <ele> (metres), <time> (ISO-8601 UTC). Cycling sensor data (power, heart rate, cadence, temperature) is not part of core GPX; it is carried in <extensions>, most commonly the Garmin TrackPointExtension (gpxtpx:hr, gpxtpx:cad) and a power element (<power> or gpxpx:PowerInWatts). Distance is not stored; it is computed from consecutive lat/lon via the haversine formula.

constraints.note: Power/HR/cadence live in <extensions>; namespaces vary by exporter, so match on local tag names.
No stored distance; compute via haversine over lat/lon.
Times are ISO-8601, usually UTC with a trailing Z.

conversion_notes.note: Map trkpt -> Trackpoint; lat/lon -> Position; ele -> AltitudeMeters; extension power -> TPX Watts.
Convert lat/lon degrees to semicircles; pack power/hr/cadence as record fields.

glossary.definition: A continuous span of track points; a new segment marks a recording gap.
Garmin's GPX extension carrying hr/cad/atemp per point.
